conversations.say: Inez, outsiders are circling the shop's account books. Before anyone starts mining old receipts for content or calling every irregular delivery a crime, we need an understanding on what neighborhood details stay protected.
conversations.offer: I will consult with you on any public choice regarding the old debt and ensure customer names remain confidential.
conversations.request: Support whatever boundary we set so that privacy isn't traded away for cheap certainty.

conversations.say: Morgan, you want a look at the ledger strip. I have transaction continuity and collector records, but I won't let my customers be labeled as R without strict terms.
conversations.offer: A bounded look at the account pattern and transaction continuity.
conversations.request: A binding promise not to call any customer R and clear terms on attribution before any wider disclosure.

conversations.say: Frankie, you appreciate a good neighborhood habit. How do you describe R's supposed private routine compared to what actually crossed my counter?
conversations.offer: Context on how routines persisted across years at the shop.
conversations.request: Describe your version of R's private habit so we can compare notes without manufacturing a fake authentication.

public_statement: Fifteen years of transactions at the bodega show routines, collectors, and changing patterns, not a simple cinematic legend. If anyone wants to talk about the shop's history, they can talk to the neighbors whose names are behind those counters first.

commitments.promise: Protect local customer privacy and consult on ledger disclosures before any external agreement.
Restrict ledger disclosure terms to pattern analysis without naming customers as R.

goal_progress: Initiated privacy alignment with Inez to shield neighborhood customers from investigative pressure.
Established initial negotiation terms with Morgan regarding ledger access and attribution boundaries.
Engaged Frankie on the private habit and continuity pattern without selling unverified conclusions.
reasoning_summary: Rafi focuses on securing neighborhood protections with Inez, setting strict disclosure boundaries and attribution terms with Morgan, and comparing routine observations with Frankie while keeping the ledger's ambiguities intact.
